notes: Built only from files uploaded in this conversation.
Gold outputs are reference answers for recommendation-style evaluation; model outputs do not need to match wording exactly.
Dataset IDs are included only when a matching uploaded metadata record could be identified confidently.

source_paper.title: Evaluating the cumulative and time-lag effects of vegetation response to drought in Central Asia under changing environments

samples.research_question: What datasets and reference paper would be a good starting point for studying cumulative and time-lagged vegetation responses to meteorological drought in Central Asia from the satellite era onward?

gold_output.answer: A strong starting point is to pair MODIS vegetation indices with a multiscale drought index and supporting hydroclimate variables for Central Asia. The paper itself is a direct methodological reference.

recommended_datasets.reason: Used to derive NDVI and EVI for vegetation greenness, and its NIR band was used to derive NIRv.
Used as a monthly solar-induced chlorophyll fluorescence proxy for photosynthetic activity in Central Asia from 2001 to 2020.
Used to characterize meteorological drought at 1- to 12-month time scales.
Used to derive soil moisture and vapor pressure deficit as key hydroclimatic drivers.

recommended_papers.title: Evaluating the cumulative and time-lag effects of vegetation response to drought in Central Asia under changing environments
recommended_papers.reason: Direct methodological reference for cumulative and lagged drought effects, drought thresholds, and driver analysis in Central Asia.

methodology_hints: Compare vegetation indicators against SPEI across multiple accumulation windows rather than a single drought timescale.
Separate cumulative effects from lagged effects and evaluate both during the growing season.
Use an explicit thresholding method for vegetation loss and analyze additional drivers such as soil moisture and VPD.
uncertainty_notes: GOSIF, SPEIbase V2.7, and ERA5-Land are named in the paper, but no matching uploaded metadata record was identified for them.

evidence.note: The study evaluated 2001-2020 vegetation response in Central Asia using NDVI, EVI, NIRv, SIF, and SPEI.
NDVI and EVI came from MOD13A1; SIF came from GOSIF; SPEI came from SPEIbase V2.7; soil moisture and VPD were derived from ERA5-Land.
Uploaded metadata contains modis-13A1-061 for MODIS vegetation indices.

source_paper.title: Diverse responses of vegetation growth to meteorological drought across climate zones and land biomes in northern China from 1981 to 2014

samples.research_question: Which datasets and reference paper should I use to analyze how vegetation in northern China responds to meteorological drought across climate zones and biomes over 1981-2014?

gold_output.answer: A suitable setup combines a long NDVI record with gridded climate inputs to compute SPEI, plus land cover and topographic information to compare biome and climate-zone differences.

recommended_datasets.reason: Primary long-term vegetation record used from 1981 to 2014 at 8 km resolution.

recommended_datasets.dataset_name: China meteorological station temperature and precipitation data
recommended_datasets.reason: Used to compute SPEI across 1-48 month time scales.

recommended_datasets.dataset_name: MODIS Land Surface Temperature / MOD11A2 or MOD11C3 family
recommended_datasets.reason: Used to evaluate interpolated climate layers and support climate characterization.
Used to validate interpolated precipitation layers.
Provided topographic information for spatial climate interpolation.

recommended_papers.title: Diverse responses of vegetation growth to meteorological drought across climate zones and land biomes in northern China from 1981 to 2014
recommended_papers.reason: Direct empirical reference for NDVI-SPEI relationships, drought timescales, and biome contrasts in northern China.

methodology_hints: Use a long NDVI time series and compute SPEI over multiple accumulation windows rather than a single drought index timescale.
Correlate NDVI with SPEI by biome and climate zone to identify drought-sensitive ecosystem types.
Keep land cover and topography in the pipeline to interpret spatial heterogeneity.
uncertainty_notes: The primary NDVI and climate inputs named in the paper do not all have matching uploaded metadata records.

required_datasets: China meteorological station temperature and precipitation data
acceptable_datasets: MODIS Land Surface Temperature / MOD11A2 or MOD11C3 family

evidence.note: The paper analyzed northern China from 1981 to 2014 using GIMMS NDVI3g and SPEI.
Monthly temperature and precipitation from 350 stations were used to compute SPEI; MODIS LST, TRMM, SRTM, and MODIS land cover supported validation and interpretation.
Uploaded metadata contains modis-11A2-061 for MODIS land surface temperature.

source_paper.title: Response of vegetation to drought in the Tibetan Plateau: Elevation differentiation and the dominant factors

samples.research_question: What datasets and reference paper would you recommend for studying how vegetation on the Tibetan Plateau responds to drought across elevation gradients?

gold_output.answer: A strong design uses multiple vegetation products together with multiscale drought indicators and elevation-related control variables so that drought responses can be compared across altitude bands.

recommended_datasets.reason: Used for EVI, one of the core vegetation indicators.
Used for LAI as a canopy-structure indicator.
Used for GPP as a productivity indicator.
Used to compute SPI and SPEI over the Tibetan Plateau.

recommended_datasets.dataset_name: Soil moisture dataset of China based on microwave data assimilation
recommended_datasets.reason: Used as a control factor for vegetation response to drought.

recommended_papers.title: Response of vegetation to drought in the Tibetan Plateau: Elevation differentiation and the dominant factors
recommended_papers.reason: Direct reference for elevation-dependent drought response and dominant-factor analysis.

methodology_hints: Use more than one vegetation indicator so drought response is not inferred from a single proxy.
Compute SPI and SPEI and compare drought-response metrics across elevation intervals.
Add control factors such as LST, soil moisture, snow cover, and land cover when interpreting spatial heterogeneity.
uncertainty_notes: Only the MODIS products listed above were matched to uploaded metadata; the forcing and soil-moisture datasets were identified only from the paper text.

acceptable_datasets: Soil moisture dataset of China based on microwave data assimilation

evidence.note: The study used EVI, LAI, and GPP from MODIS plus SPI and SPEI to assess drought response on the Tibetan Plateau from 2000 to 2015.
It also used China Meteorological Forcing data, microwave-assimilation soil moisture, elevation, LST, snow cover, and land cover as control variables.
Uploaded metadata contains modis-13A1-061, modis-15A2H-061, and modis-17A2H-061.

source_paper.title: Vegetation Responses to 2012-2016 Drought in Northern and Southern California

samples.research_question: What datasets and reference paper should I use to examine how vegetation in Northern versus Southern California responded to the 2012-2016 drought?

gold_output.answer: A suitable setup uses a consistent NDVI series for California together with a drought-severity index and supporting regional climate information to compare NorCal and SoCal responses.

recommended_datasets.reason: Used to derive monthly NDVI for California from 2000 to 2018.
Used as the main climatological drought-severity index.
Used to derive temperature anomalies for California.
Used to overlay ecological systems and vegetation zones.

recommended_papers.title: Vegetation Responses to 2012-2016 Drought in Northern and Southern California
recommended_papers.reason: Direct empirical reference for contrasting NorCal and SoCal vegetation responses during the recent California drought.

methodology_hints: Derive monthly NDVI consistently over the study region and compare anomalies against a non-drought baseline.
Pair NDVI with a drought severity index such as PDSIsc to compare vegetation sensitivity by region or vegetation zone.
Separate Northern and Southern California because hydroclimatic forcing differs strongly between them.
uncertainty_notes: The uploaded metadata clearly covers MCD43A4 but not the PDSIsc, PRISM, or USGS GAP inputs named in the paper.

evidence.note: The paper derived monthly NDVI from MCD43A4 and paired it with PDSIsc and PRISM temperature data to study 2012-2016 drought impacts.
USGS GAP vegetation overlays were used to interpret patterns across California vegetation communities.

source_paper.title: Spatiotemporal characterization of droughts and vegetation response in Northwest Africa from 1981 to 2020

samples.research_question: Which datasets and reference paper should I start with to study drought characteristics and vegetation response in Northwest Africa from 1981 to 2020?

gold_output.answer: A good starting point is to pair a long precipitation product with a long NDVI record, then compute an SPI timescale appropriate for seasonal vegetation response and add land cover and soil moisture for interpretation.

recommended_datasets.reason: Used to calculate SPI-3 and characterize drought conditions from 1981 to 2020.
Used as the vegetation health proxy across the full 1981-2020 period.
Used to support interpretation of vegetation and drought dynamics.
Used to classify land cover types in the study region.

recommended_papers.title: Spatiotemporal characterization of droughts and vegetation response in Northwest Africa from 1981 to 2020
recommended_papers.reason: Direct empirical reference for CHIRPS-SPI and AVHRR-NDVI analysis in Northwest Africa.

methodology_hints: Use a precipitation-driven drought index aligned to the agricultural or vegetation timescale of interest.
Convert the AVHRR NDVI series to monthly composites before correlating it with drought conditions.
Stratify analysis by land cover because crops and grasslands can respond differently.
uncertainty_notes: The datasets named in this paper were identified from the PDF, but matching uploaded metadata records were not found for CHIRPS, AVHRR NDVI, or FLDAS.

evidence.note: The study used CHIRPS to compute SPI-3 and AVHRR NDVI v5 to assess vegetation response from 1981 to 2020.
MODIS land cover and FLDAS soil moisture were additional supporting datasets.
